description_long: This is a headless stone statue of a human figure, likely male, depicted in a striding pose with the left leg forward. The figure is bare-chested and wears a short, pleated kilt that is characteristic of ancient Egyptian attire. The arms are held close to the sides, with the hands clenched into fists. The back of the statue features a prominent inscribed pillar, which extends from the base to the neck, likely containing hieroglyphic text. The surface of the sculpture is weathered, with a matte, slightly rough finish, and the color is a uniform light gray. The statue stands on a rectangular base, which appears to be part of the same stone block.
description: A headless, striding stone statue of a bare-chested human figure in a pleated kilt, with an inscribed pillar on its back and a weathered gray surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the striding pose, bare chest, pleated kilt, and missing head.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the muscular torso, arm, and the thickness of the inscribed pillar on the back.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the full length of the inscribed pillar with visible text and the back of the legs.
description_view_3: Right side view showing the right arm, the side of the kilt, and the pillar's attachment to the back.
attribute_subject_matter: human figure in ancient attire with inscribed pillar
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
